---
title: "Data in Quarto"
subtitle: "Code Chunks, Loading Data, and Inline Reporting"
author: "Daniela Palleschi"
date: "2026-03-25"
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
day: "Day 1"
---

## Overview

::: incremental
- Code chunks: structure and syntax
- Chunk options: controlling output
- Loading data with `here::here()`
- Saving and loading processed objects
- Inline reporting
:::

---

# Code chunks {background-color="#8D203D" data-stack-name="Code chunks"}

## Structure
````markdown
```{{r}}
rt <- c(312, 287, 445, 301, 389)
mean(rt)
```
````
````{r}
#| echo: false
rt <- c(312, 287, 445, 301, 389)
mean(rt)
````

. . .

Each chunk is executed in sequence when the document renders — output is inserted at that position.

## Languages

::: incremental
- R: `{r}`
- Python: `{python}`
- Julia: `{julia}`
- Observable JS: `{ojs}`
:::

. . .

Different languages can coexist in the same document — each runs in its own engine. `reticulate` is only needed to share objects between R and Python chunks.

## Chunk options

Options are set with the `#|` prefix:
````markdown
```{{r}}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| label: tbl-desc
#| tbl-cap: "Descriptive statistics."
```
````

. . .

| Option | Effect |
|---|---|
| `echo` | show or hide code |
| `output` | show or hide output |
| `eval` | run or skip chunk |
| `include` | include chunk and output |
| `warning` | show or hide warnings |
| `message` | show or hide messages |

## Global defaults

Set once in YAML, override per chunk:
````yaml
execute:
  echo: false      # hide code from output
  warning: false   # suppress warnings
  message: false   # suppress messages
````

. . .

Override for a specific chunk:
````markdown
```{{r}}
#| echo: true
#| label: tbl-desc
#| tbl-cap: "Descriptive statistics."
tbl_desc |> gt()
```
````

## {background-color="#8D203D"}

::: {style="text-align: center; margin-top: 1em; color: white;"}
### 💬 `echo: true` or `false`?

*Teaching materials* — `echo: true` so participants see the code

. . .

*Manuscript submission* — `echo: false` for clean output

. . .

*Internal analysis report* — `echo: true` for auditability and reproducibility

:::

## Chunk labels

Every figure or table chunk should have a label with the correct prefix:
````markdown
```{{r}}
#| label: fig-rt       # figures must start with fig-
#| label: tbl-desc     # tables must start with tbl-
```
````

. . .

::: callout-warning
Duplicate labels cause render errors. If you copy a chunk, always update the label.
:::

## {background-color="#8D203D"}

::: {style="text-align: center; margin-top: 1em; color: white;"}
### ✏️ Exercise 1 — 5 minutes

Open `exercises/day1.qmd`

1. Add a code chunk that computes `2 + 2` — render
2. Change the options so the code is shown but not evaluated — render
3. Add another chunk with `10 * 10` — set it so neither code nor output appears — render

:::

---

# Loading data {background-color="#8D203D" data-stack-name="Loading data"}

## With here::here()

Always use `here::here()` for file paths — portable across machines and operating systems:
````r
df <- readr::read_csv(here::here("data", "raw", "your-data.csv"))
````

. . .

For processed data and saved model objects:
````r
df_clean <- readRDS(here::here("data", "processed", "data_clean.rds"))
m1       <- readRDS(here::here("output", "models", "m1.rds"))
````

. . .

::: callout-tip
Load all data in a single setup chunk at the top of the document with `#| output: false`.
:::

## Saving processed data

Save processed objects so subsequent documents don't need to re-run the full pipeline:
````r
saveRDS(df_clean, here::here("data", "processed", "data_clean.rds"))
````

. . .

::: callout-note
This is the link between your analysis scripts and your manuscript. The analysis script saves; the manuscript loads. The manuscript never re-runs the analysis.
:::

## {background-color="#8D203D"}

::: {style="text-align: center; margin-top: 1em; color: white;"}
### ✏️ Exercise 2 — 5 minutes

Open `exercises/day1.qmd`

1. Add a chunk that loads the sample dataset
2. Set chunk options so the code is not printed but the chunk runs
3. Render and check there are no errors

:::

---

# Inline reporting {background-color="#8D203D" data-stack-name="Inline reporting"}

## The idea

Instead of typing numbers manually:

> "The sample comprised **48** participants."

. . .

Compute and embed directly in prose:
````markdown
The sample comprised ` r dplyr::n_distinct(df$px)` participants.
````

. . .

When data changes, re-render — the number updates automatically.

## Store values as objects

Compute once, use everywhere:
````r
n_px  <- dplyr::n_distinct(df_clean$px)
n_obs <- nrow(df_clean |> filter(region == "verb"))
m_tt  <- round(mean(df_clean$tt, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
sd_tt <- round(sd(df_clean$tt,   na.rm = TRUE), 1)
````

. . .

Then in prose:
````markdown
The sample comprised ` r n_px` participants 
(*N* observations = ` r n_obs`).
Mean total reading time was ` r m_tt` ms 
(*SD* = ` r sd_tt`).
````

## APA 7 formatting

::: incremental
- Italicise statistical symbols: `*M*`, `*SD*`, `*t*`, `*p*`
- No zero before decimal for values that cannot exceed 1: `*p* = .031` not `*p* = 0.031`
- Round consistently: `round(..., 1)` for ms, `round(..., 2)` for correlations
:::

. . .
````r
fmt_p <- function(p) {
  ifelse(p < .001, "< .001", paste0("= ", round(p, 3)))
}
````
````markdown
The effect was significant (*p* ` r fmt_p(p_value)`).
````

## {background-color="#8D203D"}

::: {style="text-align: center; margin-top: 1em; color: white;"}
### ✏️ Exercise 3 — 10 minutes

Open `exercises/day1.qmd`

1. Create a chunk that computes `m_gaze` and `sd_gaze` — mean and SD of `gaze` at the verb region, rounded to 1 decimal
2. Report these values inline in a sentence
3. Render and check the values appear correctly

. . .

**Stretch:** Add `eval: false` globally in the YAML. What breaks? How do you fix it?
:::

## {background-color="#8D203D"}

::: {style="text-align: center; margin-top: 1em; color: white;"}
### 💬 Debrief

*Did your inline values update when you changed the filter?*

. . .

*What happened when you set `eval: false` globally?*

. . .

*Any questions before we wrap up Day 1?*
:::

## Summary

::: incremental
- Code chunks run in sequence — output is inserted at render time
- Chunk options control what is shown and what is run
- `here::here()` makes file paths portable
- Save processed objects with `saveRDS()`, load with `readRDS()`
- Inline R code keeps reported values in sync with your analysis
:::
